project.name: ccbot
project.description: Telegram bot that bridges Telegram Forum topics to Claude Code sessions via tmux windows — control, monitor, and interact with AI coding sessions remotely.

nodes.name: bot.py
nodes.summary: Main bot entry point — registers all Telegram command, callback, and message handlers, manages the bot lifecycle via post_init/post_shutdown, and routes messages between Telegram forum topics and tmux windows running Claude Code sessions. Also handles voice transcription, photo forwarding, screenshot capture, and interactive UI navigation.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses python-telegram-bot v20+ Application/Handler pattern with AIORateLimiter. Defines ~20 async handler functions. Hot path (text_handler) must never abort after tmux injection succeeds.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Loads configuration from environment variables with .env file support (local .env > CCBOT_DIR/.env). Exposes a singleton Config instance and scrubs sensitive env vars to prevent credential leaks to child processes.
nodes.languageNotes: Imports dotenv.load_dotenv for .env loading. Scrub loop removes TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN/ALLOWED_USERS/OPENAI_API_KEY from os.environ after capture.

nodes.name: callback_data.py
nodes.summary: Defines all CB_* prefix constants for Telegram inline keyboard callback data routing. Each constant encodes a distinct action: history pagination, directory browser navigation, window picker binding, screenshot refresh, interactive UI navigation, and session picking.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure constant definitions with no imports beyond stdlib. Callback payloads must stay under Telegram's 64-byte limit.

nodes.name: directory_browser.py
nodes.summary: Builds inline keyboard UIs for three flows: the directory browser (navigate filesystem to create a new session), the window picker (bind an existing unbound tmux window), and the session picker (resume an existing Claude session). Includes pagination, state keys, and clear-state helpers.
nodes.languageNotes: State keys (STATE_KEY/BROWSE_PATH_KEY/etc.) shared with bot.py via direct import. UiState machine transitions: idle -> browsing_directory -> selecting_session -> create_window.

nodes.name: monitor_state.py
nodes.summary: Persistent state tracker for the session monitor — stores per-session byte offsets (TrackedSession) to enable incremental JSONL reading across restarts. Wraps load/save in a MonitorState dataclass with dirty-flag optimization.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses dataclasses for TrackedSession/MonitorState. Delegates atomic JSON writes to utils.atomic_write_json. Byte-offset tracking avoids re-reading already-processed messages.

nodes.name: screenshot.py
nodes.summary: Renders captured tmux pane text (with ANSI color codes) into a dark-background PNG image using Pillow. Features a three-tier font fallback chain (JetBrains Mono, Noto Sans Mono CJK SC, Symbola) for broad character coverage. ANSI parsing supports 16/256/RGB colors.
nodes.languageNotes: CPU-bound rendering offloaded to thread pool via asyncio.to_thread. Font tiers selected per-codepoint. ANSI parser handles SGR codes (0-107) with extended 256-color and RGB support.

nodes.name: session_monitor.py
nodes.summary: Background async polling service that watches Claude Code JSONL session files for new messages. Scans projects directories, tracks byte offsets via MonitorState, detects session_map changes, and emits NewMessage objects through a callback. Handles file truncation, corrupted offsets, and partial writes.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses aiofiles for non-blocking file I/O. Maintains in-memory mtime cache to skip unchanged files. Pending tool_use state carried across poll cycles for tool_use/tool_result pairing.

nodes.name: transcribe.py
nodes.summary: Voice-to-text transcription via the OpenAI audio/transcriptions API using gpt-4o-transcribe model. Uses httpx directly (no OpenAI SDK) with a lazily-initialized async client singleton.
nodes.languageNotes: Single async function wrapping httpx POST to OpenAI. Client is lazily initialized singleton. close_client() called during bot shutdown.

nodes.name: utils.py
nodes.summary: Shared utility functions: ccbot_dir() resolves the config directory from CCBOT_DIR env var, atomic_write_json() performs crash-safe JSON writes via temp-file-then-rename, and read_cwd_from_jsonl() extracts the cwd from the first entry of a JSONL file.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure Python stdlib (json, os, tempfile, pathlib). atomic_write_json uses os.replace for atomic cross-filesystem renames.

nodes.name: Config
nodes.summary: Application configuration singleton — loads TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, ALLOWED_USERS, TMUX_SESSION_NAME, CLAUDE_COMMAND, monitoring intervals, OpenAI keys, and display preferences from environment variables or .env files.
nodes.languageNotes: Instantiated at module level as `config`. is_user_allowed() is the only method beyond __init__.

nodes.name: TrackedSession
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a single tracked Claude Code session with session_id, file_path, and last_byte_offset for incremental JSONL reading. Provides to_dict/from_dict serialization.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses @dataclass decorator with field defaults. to_dict() delegates to dataclasses.asdict.

nodes.name: MonitorState
nodes.summary: Persistent state container for the session monitor — manages a dict of TrackedSession objects keyed by session_id, with load/save/save_if_dirty lifecycle and dirty-flag optimization.
nodes.languageNotes: Dirty flag avoids unnecessary writes. Atomic save via utils.atomic_write_json. load() handles missing/empty files gracefully.

nodes.name: TextStyle
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing ANSI text styling with foreground color and optional background color. Defaults to light gray on dark gray.

nodes.name: StyledSegment
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a text segment with its TextStyle and font_tier index for multi-font rendering.

nodes.name: SessionInfo
nodes.summary: Dataclass holding session_id and file_path for a Claude Code session discovered during project scanning.

nodes.name: NewMessage
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a new message detected by the monitor, with fields for session_id, text, completion status, content_type, tool_use_id, role, tool_name, and optional image data from tool_result images.

nodes.name: SessionMonitor
nodes.summary: Primary monitoring service — runs an async polling loop that scans Claude Code projects, reads new JSONL entries via byte-offset tracking, parses them through TranscriptParser, and emits NewMessage objects. Detects session_map changes and cleans up stale sessions on startup.
nodes.languageNotes: Core loop in _monitor_loop(). Uses aiofiles for non-blocking reads. Maintains per-session pending tool state across cycles. mtime cache for quick no-change detection.

nodes.name: is_user_allowed
nodes.summary: Checks if a Telegram user ID is in the configured allowed_users set.

nodes.name: start_command
nodes.summary: /start handler — shows welcome message after authorizing the user.

nodes.name: history_command
nodes.summary: /history handler — shows paginated message history for the bound session.

nodes.name: screenshot_command
nodes.summary: /screenshot handler — captures the tmux pane content as a PNG image with an inline keyboard for terminal control keys.

nodes.name: unbind_command
nodes.summary: /unbind handler — unbinds the current topic from its tmux window without killing the Claude session.

nodes.name: esc_command
nodes.summary: /esc handler — sends the Escape control character to the tmux pane to interrupt Claude.

nodes.name: usage_command
nodes.summary: /usage handler — sends /usage to Claude Code, captures the terminal output, and parses structured usage info or falls back to raw pane text.

nodes.name: topic_closed_handler
nodes.summary: Handles forum topic closure — kills the associated tmux window and cleans up all per-topic state.

nodes.name: topic_edited_handler
nodes.summary: Handles forum topic rename — syncs the new topic name to the tmux window name and internal SessionManager state.

nodes.name: forward_command_handler
nodes.summary: Forwards any unknown /command (e.g. /clear, /compact, /cost) to Claude Code as a slash command via tmux, enabling remote Claude interaction.

nodes.name: unsupported_content_handler
nodes.summary: Replies to unsupported media types (stickers, video, etc.) with a warning message listing supported types.

nodes.name: photo_handler
nodes.summary: Downloads incoming photos to ~/.ccbot/images/ and forwards the file path to Claude Code via tmux, optionally with a text caption.

nodes.name: voice_handler
nodes.summary: Downloads voice messages, transcribes them via OpenAI API, and forwards the transcribed text to Claude Code.

nodes.name: text_handler
nodes.summary: Primary text message handler — routes user text to a bound tmux window, or shows the window picker/directory browser for unbound topics. Handles state machine transitions, bash command capture, and interactive UI pre-checks.
nodes.languageNotes: Hot path: must never abort after tmux injection. Manages three UI state machines (window-picker, directory-browser, session-picker). Triggers background bash output capture for !-prefixed commands.

nodes.name: callback_handler
nodes.summary: Central callback query dispatcher — routes all inline keyboard interactions (directory browser, window picker, session picker, screenshot keys, history pagination, and interactive UI navigation) based on CB_* prefix constants.
nodes.languageNotes: Single dispatcher function matching ~20 callback prefixes. Validates topic binding freshness to prevent stale interactions. Handles both old and new history pagination formats.

nodes.name: handle_new_message
nodes.summary: Callback invoked by SessionMonitor for each new Claude message — routes to the correct user topic, handles interactive UI tools specially, filters by CCBOT_SHOW_TOOL_CALLS, and enqueues content messages for sequential delivery.
nodes.languageNotes: Checks interactive tools first before normal content path. Builds response parts via build_response_parts, then enqueues via enqueue_content_message. Updates user read offsets after delivery.

nodes.name: post_init
nodes.summary: Bot startup lifecycle hook — sets bot commands, resolves stale window IDs, pre-fills the rate limiter bucket, starts the SessionMonitor and the status polling loop.

nodes.name: post_shutdown
nodes.summary: Bot shutdown lifecycle hook — cancels status polling, shuts down message queue workers, stops the session monitor, and closes the transcribe HTTP client.

nodes.name: create_bot
nodes.summary: Application factory — builds a Telegram Application with AIORateLimiter, post_init/post_shutdown hooks, and all command/callback/message handlers registered.

nodes.name: clear_browse_state
nodes.summary: Clears all directory browsing state keys from user_data.

nodes.name: clear_window_picker_state
nodes.summary: Clears window picker state keys from user_data.

nodes.name: clear_session_picker_state
nodes.summary: Clears session picker state keys from user_data.

nodes.name: build_window_picker
nodes.summary: Builds an inline keyboard UI listing unbound tmux windows for quick binding, with New Session and Cancel buttons.

nodes.name: build_directory_browser
nodes.summary: Builds a paginated inline keyboard directory browser UI with folder navigation, Select, Cancel, and page controls.

nodes.name: build_session_picker
nodes.summary: Builds an inline keyboard UI for selecting an existing Claude session to resume, with relative time and message count info.

nodes.name: text_to_image
nodes.summary: Renders monospace terminal text (with optional ANSI color codes) onto a dark-background PNG image using a three-tier font fallback chain. Offloads CPU-bound Pillow rendering to a thread pool.

nodes.name: transcribe_voice
nodes.summary: Transcribes OGG voice data to text via the OpenAI audio/transcriptions API using the gpt-4o-transcribe model.

nodes.name: close_client
nodes.summary: Closes the lazily-initialized httpx AsyncClient singleton. Called during bot shutdown.

nodes.name: ccbot_dir
nodes.summary: Returns the configuration directory path from CCBOT_DIR env var, defaulting to ~/.ccbot.

nodes.name: atomic_write_json
nodes.summary: Atomically writes JSON data to a file using a temp-file-then-rename pattern to prevent corruption.

nodes.name: read_cwd_from_jsonl
nodes.summary: Extracts the cwd field from the first JSONL entry that contains one.

nodes.name: Topic-to-Session Routing
nodes.summary: The 1:1 mapping between Telegram forum topics and tmux windows. Each topic is bound to one tmux window running one Claude Code instance, with per-user thread bindings stored in session_manager state.
nodes.languageNotes: Keyed by tmux window ID (@0, @12), not window name. No backward-compat for non-topic mode. Group chat_id required for supergroup message routing.

nodes.name: Session Monitoring via JSONL Polling
nodes.summary: Async polling loop that watches Claude Code's JSONL session files, detects new entries via byte-offset tracking, parses them through TranscriptParser, and emits NewMessage objects for delivery to users.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses aiofiles for non-blocking reads. Handles file truncation (/clear), corrupted offsets, and partial writes. Maintains per-session pending tool_use state across poll cycles.

nodes.name: Per-User Message Queue
nodes.summary: FIFO message queue per Telegram user with message merging (3800-char limit) and tool_use/tool_result pairing. Ensures status messages always appear last in the queue.

nodes.name: Callback Query Routing
nodes.summary: Central callback handler dispatches all inline keyboard interactions by matching CB_* prefix constants. Validates topic binding freshness and supports both old/new callback data formats.
nodes.languageNotes: Callback payloads must stay under Telegram's 64-byte limit. Prefix-based dispatching with topic-mismatch validation.

nodes.name: Directory Browser UI
nodes.summary: Paginated inline keyboard interface for navigating the filesystem to select a working directory for a new Claude Code session. Supports hidden directory toggling and parent directory navigation.

nodes.name: Terminal Interactive UI Handling
nodes.summary: Detects and renders terminal-based interactive UIs (permission prompts, selection menus, model pickers) as Telegram inline keyboards for remote interaction with tk or text-based UIs.

nodes.name: Screenshot Rendering
nodes.summary: Renders captured tmux pane text (with ANSI color codes) into a dark-background PNG image using Pillow with multi-font fallback support.

nodes.name: ANSI Color Parsing
nodes.summary: Parses ANSI escape code SGR sequences (16 colors, 256-color palette, and full RGB) into TextStyle objects for rendering. Handles 0-107 color codes with bold/bright variants.

nodes.name: Voice Message Transcription
nodes.summary: Transcribes OGG voice messages to text via the OpenAI audio/transcriptions API using gpt-4o-transcribe, then forwards the result to Claude Code.

nodes.name: State Persistence
nodes.summary: Crash-safe persistent storage for thread bindings (state.json), session_map (session_map.json), and monitor byte offsets (monitor_state.json). Uses atomic temp-file-rename writes to prevent corruption.
nodes.languageNotes: Three state files under CCBOT_DIR. atomic_write_json ensures crash safety. Monitor state uses dirty-flag optimization.

nodes.name: Telegram API Rate Limiting
nodes.summary: Uses AIORateLimiter with max_retries=5 to handle Telegram's server-side rate limits. Pre-fills the bucket on restart to match Telegram's persisted counter.
nodes.languageNotes: AIORateLimiter from python-telegram-bot. Bucket pre-fill hack sets _level=max_rate on restart.

nodes.name: Terminal Status Polling
nodes.summary: Background polling loop that periodically checks the Claude Code terminal status line and sends/replies to users with status updates. Detects interactive UIs and idle states.

nodes.name: Environment-Based Configuration
nodes.summary: Centralized configuration loaded from environment variables with .env file support, providing bot token, user allowlist, tmux paths, monitoring intervals, and feature flags to all modules.

nodes.name: User Authorization
nodes.summary: Access control via the ALLOWED_USERS environment variable — a comma-separated list of Telegram user IDs. Every handler checks is_user_allowed() before processing.

nodes.name: Background Bash Output Capture
nodes.summary: Background asyncio task that captures the output of !-prefixed bash commands from the tmux pane, sending incremental updates to the user as a streaming message that gets edited in-place.

nodes.label: cleanup.py
nodes.summary: Unified cleanup API for topic state — coordinates state cleanup across modules when topics are deleted or become stale
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers

nodes.label: clear_topic_state
nodes.summary: Clear all memory state associated with a topic — status messages, tool message IDs, interactive UI state, pending thread state
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Cleanup

nodes.label: interactive_ui.py
nodes.summary: Interactive UI handling for Claude Code prompts — AskUserQuestion, ExitPlanMode, Permission prompts, RestoreCheckpoint with keyboard navigation via inline keyboards
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers

nodes.label: INTERACTIVE_TOOL_NAMES
nodes.summary: Frozenset of tool names that trigger interactive UI via JSONL (AskUserQuestion, ExitPlanMode)
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Interactive Ui

nodes.label: _interactive_msgs
nodes.summary: Module-level dict tracking interactive UI message IDs keyed by (user_id, thread_id_or_0)
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Interactive Ui

nodes.label: _interactive_mode
nodes.summary: Module-level dict tracking which window is in interactive mode keyed by (user_id, thread_id_or_0)
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Interactive Ui

nodes.label: get_interactive_window
nodes.summary: Get the window_id for user's interactive mode, returns None if not in interactive mode
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Interactive Ui

nodes.label: set_interactive_mode
nodes.summary: Set interactive mode for a user by storing window_id in _interactive_mode
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Interactive Ui

nodes.label: clear_interactive_mode
nodes.summary: Clear interactive mode for a user without deleting the message
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Interactive Ui

nodes.label: _build_interactive_keyboard
nodes.summary: Build inline keyboard for interactive UI navigation with directional keys, Tab, Space, Esc, Enter, Refresh; RestoreCheckpoint uses vertical-only layout
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Interactive Ui

nodes.label: handle_interactive_ui
nodes.summary: Capture terminal pane, detect interactive UI, send content to user with navigation keyboard; edits existing message or sends new one, cleans up old message on replacement
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Interactive Ui

nodes.label: clear_interactive_msg
nodes.summary: Clear tracked interactive message, delete from chat, and exit interactive mode for a user/thread
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Interactive Ui

nodes.label: message_queue.py
nodes.summary: Per-user message queue management ensuring FIFO ordering, status-after-content, message merging (3800 char limit), and thread-aware sending with tool_use/tool_result pairing and flood control
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers

nodes.label: MessageTask
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a queued message task with task_type (content/status_update/status_clear), parts, tool_use_id, content_type, thread_id, image_data
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Message Queue

nodes.label: _message_queues
nodes.summary: Module-level dict of per-user asyncio.Queues for message tasks
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Message Queue

nodes.label: _queue_workers
nodes.summary: Module-level dict of per-user asyncio.Task workers processing message queues
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Message Queue

nodes.label: _queue_locks
nodes.summary: Module-level dict of per-user asyncio.Locks protecting drain/refill operations during merge
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Message Queue

nodes.label: _tool_msg_ids
nodes.summary: Module-level dict mapping (tool_use_id, user_id, thread_id_or_0) to telegram message_id for editing tool_use messages with tool_result
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Message Queue

nodes.label: _status_msg_info
nodes.summary: Module-level dict tracking status message (message_id, window_id, last_text) keyed by (user_id, thread_id_or_0)
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Message Queue

nodes.label: _flood_until
nodes.summary: Module-level dict mapping user_id to monotonic time when flood control ban expires
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Message Queue

nodes.label: MERGE_MAX_LENGTH
nodes.summary: 3800 character limit for merging consecutive content messages, leaving room for markdown conversion overhead
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Message Queue

nodes.label: FLOOD_CONTROL_MAX_WAIT
nodes.summary: 10 seconds max wait for flood control before dropping tasks; longer bans pause the entire queue
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Message Queue

nodes.label: get_or_create_queue
nodes.summary: Get or create asyncio.Queue and worker task for a user; lazily initializes queue infrastructure
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Message Queue

nodes.label: _can_merge_tasks
nodes.summary: Check if two content tasks can be merged — same window_id, both content type, neither is tool_use or tool_result
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Message Queue

nodes.label: _merge_content_tasks
nodes.summary: Merge consecutive content tasks from queue within MERGE_MAX_LENGTH; returns merged task and merge count with proper queue counter management
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Message Queue

nodes.label: _message_queue_worker
nodes.summary: Background worker processing a user's message queue sequentially; handles flood control (drops status, waits for content), merges content tasks, and dispatches by task_type
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Message Queue

nodes.label: _process_content_task
nodes.summary: Process a content message task — edits tool_result into tool_use message, sends parts with status-to-content conversion, records tool_use IDs for later editing
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Message Queue

nodes.label: _convert_status_to_content
nodes.summary: Convert a status message to content by editing it in-place; falls back to plain text with sentinel stripping on failure
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Message Queue

nodes.label: _process_status_update_task
nodes.summary: Process status update task — creates, edits, or re-sends status messages with window-change detection and typing indicator for active Claude sessions
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Message Queue

nodes.label: _check_and_send_status
nodes.summary: Check terminal for status line after content and enqueue status message; skips if more messages are pending
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Message Queue

nodes.label: enqueue_content_message
nodes.summary: Enqueue a content message task with optional tool_use tracking, thread targeting, and image data
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Message Queue

nodes.label: enqueue_status_update
nodes.summary: Enqueue status update or clear; deduplicates against displayed text, skips during flood control, builds status_update or status_clear MessageTask
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Message Queue

nodes.label: clear_status_msg_info
nodes.summary: Clear status message tracking for a user and optionally a specific thread
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Message Queue

nodes.label: clear_tool_msg_ids_for_topic
nodes.summary: Clear all tool message ID tracking entries matching a given user and thread
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Message Queue

nodes.label: shutdown_workers
nodes.summary: Cancel all queue workers and clear queue state during bot shutdown
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Message Queue

nodes.label: status_polling.py
nodes.summary: Terminal status line polling for thread-bound windows — detects Claude Code status and interactive UIs, updates Telegram messages, polls topic existence, cleans up deleted topics
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers

nodes.label: STATUS_POLL_INTERVAL
nodes.summary: 1 second polling interval for status checks
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Status Polling

nodes.label: TOPIC_CHECK_INTERVAL
nodes.summary: 60 second interval for topic existence probes via unpin_all_forum_topic_messages
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Status Polling

nodes.label: update_status_message
nodes.summary: Poll terminal for a window: detect interactive UIs (always), check status line (unless skip_status); handles interactive mode switching and stale mode cleanup
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Status Polling

nodes.label: status_poll_loop
nodes.summary: Background polling loop: periodically probes topic existence (cleanup deleted topics), iterates all thread bindings, calls update_status_message for each, skips status when queue non-empty
nodes.name: Ccbot.Handlers.Status Polling

nodes.label: session.py
nodes.summary: Claude Code session management — the core state hub managing Window→Session and User→Thread→Window mappings, state persistence, startup re-resolution, message history, and group chat routing
nodes.name: Ccbot

nodes.label: WindowState
nodes.summary: Dataclass for persistent tmux window state — session_id, cwd, window_name with to_dict/from_dict serialization
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session

nodes.label: ClaudeSession
nodes.summary: Dataclass describing a Claude Code session — session_id, summary, message_count, file_path
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session

nodes.label: SessionManager
nodes.summary: Core singleton managing window states, thread bindings, display names, group chat routing, session persistence, and message history retrieval; provides the central coordination API for all window↔session↔topic operations
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session

nodes.label: session_manager
nodes.summary: Global SessionManager singleton instance — the central state coordinator for the entire bot
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session

nodes.label: window_states
nodes.summary: Dict mapping window_id to WindowState (session_id, cwd, window_name) — persisted in state.json
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session.Sessionmanager

nodes.label: thread_bindings
nodes.summary: Dict mapping user_id → {thread_id → window_id} — the core 1 topic = 1 window routing table
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session.Sessionmanager

nodes.label: window_display_names
nodes.summary: Dict mapping window_id to human-readable window_name for UI display
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session.Sessionmanager

nodes.label: group_chat_ids
nodes.summary: Dict mapping 'user_id:thread_id' to group chat_id (negative number) for supergroup forum topic routing
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session.Sessionmanager

nodes.label: bind_thread
nodes.summary: Bind a Telegram topic thread to a tmux window, update display name, and persist state
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session.Sessionmanager

nodes.label: unbind_thread
nodes.summary: Remove a thread binding, clean up user entry if empty, return previously bound window_id
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session.Sessionmanager

nodes.label: resolve_window_for_thread
nodes.summary: Resolve the tmux window_id for a user's thread; returns None if thread_id is None or not bound
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session.Sessionmanager

nodes.label: iter_thread_bindings
nodes.summary: Generator yielding (user_id, thread_id, window_id) tuples for all thread bindings
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session.Sessionmanager

nodes.label: resolve_session_for_window
nodes.summary: Resolve a tmux window to its ClaudeSession using persisted session_id + cwd; clears stale state if JSONL file is gone
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session.Sessionmanager

nodes.label: load_session_map
nodes.summary: Read session_map.json, update window_states with new session associations, migrate old-format keys, remove stale entries, persist changes
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session.Sessionmanager

nodes.label: resolve_stale_ids
nodes.summary: Startup re-resolution of persisted window IDs against live tmux windows — handles old-format migration (window_name→window_id) and stale ID re-resolution by display name
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session.Sessionmanager

nodes.label: find_users_for_session
nodes.summary: Find all users whose thread-bound window maps to a given session_id — uses in-memory window_states for O(bindings) lookup
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session.Sessionmanager

nodes.label: send_to_window
nodes.summary: Send text to a tmux window by ID via tmux_manager.send_keys
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session.Sessionmanager

nodes.label: get_recent_messages
nodes.summary: Get parsed user/assistant messages for a window's session with byte range filtering via TranscriptParser
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session.Sessionmanager

nodes.label: list_sessions_for_directory
nodes.summary: List existing Claude sessions for a directory by globbing JSONL files, sorted by mtime (newest first), capped at 10
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session.Sessionmanager

nodes.label: resolve_chat_id
nodes.summary: Resolve correct chat_id for sending messages — returns group chat_id for forum topics, user_id for private chats
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session.Sessionmanager

nodes.label: set_group_chat_id
nodes.summary: Store group chat_id for supergroup forum topic routing — critical for outbound messages to forum topics
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session.Sessionmanager

nodes.label: get_display_name
nodes.summary: Get display name for a window_id, fallback to window_id itself
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session.Sessionmanager

nodes.label: update_display_name
nodes.summary: Update the display name for a window and persist to state.json
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session.Sessionmanager

nodes.label: override_session_map_entry
nodes.summary: Force a window's session_map entry to a specific session_id (used after --resume); creates entry if missing, no-op if consistent
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session.Sessionmanager

nodes.label: wait_for_session_map_entry
nodes.summary: Poll session_map.json until an entry for window_id appears (with timeout), then load into window_states
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session.Sessionmanager

nodes.label: get_window_state
nodes.summary: Get or create WindowState for a window_id
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session.Sessionmanager

nodes.label: clear_window_session
nodes.summary: Clear session association for a window, persist state
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session.Sessionmanager

nodes.label: update_user_window_offset
nodes.summary: Update the user's last read byte offset for a window, persist state
nodes.name: Ccbot.Session.Sessionmanager

nodes.label: terminal_parser.py
nodes.summary: Terminal output parser detecting Claude Code UI elements in pane text — interactive UIs (AskUserQuestion, ExitPlanMode, Permission, RestoreCheckpoint, BashApproval, Settings) and status line (spinner + working text)
nodes.name: Ccbot

nodes.label: InteractiveUIContent
nodes.summary: Dataclass for extracted interactive UI content — content text and pattern name (e.g. AskUserQuestion)

nodes.label: UIPattern

nodes.summary: JSONL transcript parser for Claude Code session files. Parses session JSONL lines into structured messages. Handles text, thinking, tool_use, tool_result, local_command, and user messages. Core classes: TranscriptParser (static methods), ParsedEntry, ParsedMessage, PendingToolInfo. Tool pairing: matches tool_use with tool_result via tool_use_id. Defines sentinel tokens for expandable blockquotes. Used by both session.py (history) and session_monitor.py (real-time).
nodes.name: Transcript Parser

nodes.summary: Message splitting utility for Telegram's 4096-character limit. Provides split_message() which splits long text into Telegram-safe chunks, preferring newline boundaries and preserving code block integrity. Uses UTF-16 code unit length for accurate Telegram accounting. Handles forceful splitting of overlong lines and code block fence re-opening across chunks.
nodes.name: Telegram Sender

nodes.summary: Markdown to Telegram MarkdownV2 conversion layer. Wraps telegramify_markdown with custom rendering rules. Converts markdown tables to card-style key-value format (convert_markdown_tables). Handles expandable blockquotes delimited by TranscriptParser sentinel tokens (rendered as >...|| syntax with truncation). Escapes special characters for MarkdownV2. Disables indented code blocks, only fenced blocks are treated as code.
nodes.name: Markdown V2

nodes.summary: Response message building for Telegram delivery. build_response_parts() creates paginated response messages from Claude Code output. Handles different content types: user messages (emoji prefix), thinking (truncated, with expandable quote), and assistant text. Splits long messages into pages within Telegram's limit, adding [1/N] suffixes. Preserves expandable quote atomicity (no splitting inside quotes). Converts tables to card-style before splitting.
nodes.name: Response Builder

nodes.summary: Safe message sending helpers with MarkdownV2 fallback. Provides safe_reply, safe_edit, safe_send, send_with_fallback, and send_photo. Each method attempts formatted MarkdownV2 send first, falls back to plain text on failure. Strips expandable quote sentinel markers in fallback mode. Photo sending supports single images and media groups. RetryAfter exceptions are re-raised for caller handling.
nodes.name: Message Sender

nodes.summary: Message history display with pagination. send_history() sends or edits message history for a window's session with page navigation. Supports both full history and unread message range views (via start_byte/end_byte). Filters messages based on config.show_user_messages. Formats messages with timestamps, role prefixes, and expandable quote sentinel stripping. _build_history_keyboard() creates inline keyboard with Older/Newer page buttons. Updates user read offset after viewing unread.
nodes.name: History

nodes.label: Check
nodes.content: CI lint/typecheck workflow. Runs on push and PR to main. Matrix across Python 3.12/3.13. Steps: checkout, setup-uv, uv sync, ruff format check, ruff lint, pyright typecheck, pytest.
nodes.name: Check

nodes.label: Claude Code Review
nodes.content: AI code review workflow. Triggered by pull_request_target on opened/synchronize/ready_for_review/reopened. Runs anthropics/claude-code-action@v1 with code-review plugin to review PR diffs.
nodes.name: Claude-Code-Review

nodes.label: Claude Code
nodes.content: AI assistant workflow. Triggered by issue_comment, PR review comment, issues, PR review (filtered by @claude mention). Runs anthropics/claude-code-action@v1 to let Claude interact on issues/PRs.
nodes.name: Claude

nodes.title: System Architecture
nodes.summary: Defines the complete system architecture of the ccbot Telegram bot, showing all modules, their relationships, and key design decisions for topic-based routing of Claude Code sessions via tmux.
key_topics: topic-based routing (1 topic = 1 window = 1 session)
module relationships (bot.py, tmux_manager.py, session_monitor.py, terminal_parser.py, hook.py, session.py)
MarkdownV2 output with auto fallback
terminal_parser for interactive UI detection
hook-based session tracking via session_map.json
per-user message queue + worker
tool_use ↔ tool_result pairing across poll cycles
window ID-centric state (not window name)
startup re-resolution of stale window IDs
no truncation at parse layer, split at send layer
nodes.name: Doc-Architecture

nodes.title: Message Handling
nodes.summary: Describes the per-user message queue architecture, message merging strategy, status message handling, rate limiting with AIORateLimiter, and performance optimizations for monitoring.
key_topics: FIFO per-user message queues with worker pattern
message merging (3800 char limit, tool_use/tool_result break chain)
status message editing into first content message
AIORateLimiter (max_retries=5, 30/s global, pre-filled bucket on restart)
background status polling at 1s intervals
mtime cache for unchanged file skipping
byte offset incremental reads with truncation detection
split_message at 4096 char Telegram limit
nodes.name: Doc-Message-Handling

nodes.title: Topic-Only Architecture
nodes.summary: Defines the strict 1 Topic = 1 Window = 1 Session mapping, including thread_bindings and session_map.json, message flows, topic lifecycle, and session lifecycle management.
key_topics: exclusive Telegram Forum (topics) mode — no backward compat
1 Topic = 1 Window = 1 Session invariant
Mapping 1: thread_bindings (user_id → thread_id → window_id)
Mapping 2: session_map.json (window_id → session_id, written by hook)
outbound message flow (user → tmux window)
inbound message flow (session monitor → user topic)
new topic flow with directory browser and session picker
resume session flow with override_session_map_entry
topic lifecycle (close/delete auto-kills window, unbinds thread)
startup cleanup of orphaned sessions
runtime change detection for session_map changes
nodes.name: Doc-Topic-Architecture

properties.summary: SessionStart hook writes session_map.json; monitor polls it to detect session changes across restarts
Per-user FIFO message queue with merging (3800 char limit) and tool_use/tool_result pairing
AIORateLimiter(max_retries=5) on the Application (30/s global); pre-filled bucket on restart to avoid Telegram server burst
HTML via chatgpt-md-converter with plain text fallback; no runtime switch to MarkdownV2; HTML-aware splitting for long messages
Interactive inline keyboards for AskUserQuestion, ExitPlanMode, Permission Prompts; callback data under 64 bytes
state.json (thread bindings), session_map.json (hook-generated), monitor_state.json (byte offsets) — survive restarts
Voice messages transcribed via OpenAI API and forwarded as text to Claude Code
python-telegram-bot based bot operating in Telegram Forum (topics) mode; command handlers and topic routing
Window management via libtmux; all internal routing keyed by tmux window ID; window names as display labels
Bridges Claude Code CLI sessions running in tmux to Telegram topics; reads output and sends keystrokes
Polls JSONL transcript files every 2s; detects and notifies on assistant responses, thinking content, tool use/result, and local command output
Inline keyboard UI for browsing project directories and resuming or creating new Claude sessions
Captures terminal text as PNG image with ANSI color support using bundled fonts
Comma-separated ALLOWED_USERS env var; only authorized Telegram user IDs can interact with the bot
Install via uv tool, pipx from GitHub, or source clone with uv sync
Config directory ~/.ccbot/ (overridable via CCBOT_DIR); .env loading priority: local .env > config dir .env

tour.title: Project Overview — What CCBot Does
tour.description: Start here to understand the big picture. CCBot bridges Telegram Forum topics to Claude Code sessions running inside tmux windows. Each Telegram topic maps 1:1 to a tmux window running a Claude Code instance. Read the README first for the user-facing story, then AGENTS.md for the technical design constraints and architectural rules that govern the codebase (1 topic = 1 window, topic-only mode, hook-based session tracking, MarkdownV2-only).

tour.title: Entry Point & Configuration Bootstrap
tour.description: The CLI dispatcher at src/ccbot/main.py handles two modes: `ccbot hook` (delegates to hook.py for Claude Code SessionStart processing) and the default mode, which configures logging, validates config, ensures a tmux session exists, and calls bot.create_bot(). Config loads from environment variables with `.env` file support; the module-level `config` singleton is imported by nearly every other module. Understanding the two execution modes is critical — the hook runs as a child process inside Claude Code, while the bot runs as a long-lived daemon.

tour.title: Bot Application Core — Routing & Lifecycle
tour.description: The heart of the system at src/ccbot/bot.py. This file registers all Telegram command/callback/message handlers and manages the bot lifecycle via python-telegram-bot's Application. Key patterns: `text_handler` is the main message routing function — it checks authorization, resolves thread→window bindings, shows window pickers or directory browsers for unbound topics, and forwards messages to Claude Code via tmux. Topic-closed and topic-edited handlers sync Telegram topic lifecycle to tmux windows. The `_create_and_bind_window` helper is shared across directory browser, session picker, and window picker flows.

tour.title: Session & State Management — The Routing Backbone
tour.description: SessionManager (singleton in session.py) is the state hub: it manages window→session mappings (which Claude session_id a tmux window holds), user→thread→window bindings (topic routing), per-user read offsets, group chat ID mapping for supergroup routing, and state persistence to state.json. The session_map.json file (written by Claude Code's SessionStart hook) drives the session monitor. The session_monitor.py runs a background polling loop that watches JSONL transcript files via byte-offset tracking and emits NewMessage objects. TranscriptParser parses the JSONL files into display-ready entries, handling tool_use/tool_result pairing, thinking blocks, and expandable quotes. Follow the data flow: hook writes session_map → monitor polls and reads JSONL → TranscriptParser parses → messages routed to bound topics.

tour.title: Terminal & Tmux Integration — The Bridge Layer
tour.description: TmuxManager wraps libtmux to provide async-friendly operations on a single tmux session: listing windows, capturing pane content (with or without ANSI colors), sending keystrokes, creating/killing windows. The `send_keys` method has special handling for Claude Code's TUI (e.g., delayed Enter for `!` bash commands). terminal_parser.py extracts status lines, interactive UI content (ask questions, permission prompts), and bash output from captured pane text. screenshot.py renders terminal content to PNG images. Together these files form the bridge layer: TmuxManager talks to tmux, terminal_parser interprets what's on screen, and the bot layer feeds this to Telegram.

tour.title: Message Processing Pipeline — Queue, Format, Send
tour.description: Messages flow through a structured pipeline. telegram_sender.py splits long messages at 4096 chars. markdown_v2.py converts markdown to Telegram's MarkdownV2 with automatic fallback to plain text. response_builder.py builds paginated response parts per content type (text, thinking, tool_use, tool_result) with truncation and page numbering. The message_queue.py implements per-user FIFO queues with background workers that merge consecutive content messages (up to 3800 chars), handle tool_use/tool_result editing (editing tool_use messages in-place with results), track status messages (single ephemeral message per user+thread), and implement flood control with RetryAfter handling. The queue ensures ordered delivery without overwhelming Telegram's rate limits.

tour.title: Handler Layer — Interactive UI, Polling & Session Management
tour.description: The handler modules implement the interactive features. interactive_ui.py captures Claude Code's terminal UIs (AskUserQuestion, ExitPlanMode, Permission Prompts) and renders them with inline keyboard navigation (arrows, enter, esc, tab). status_polling.py runs a 1-second background loop that polls terminal status lines for all bound threads, detects interactive UIs in real-time, and probes topic existence every 60 seconds to clean up deleted topics. directory_browser.py provides an inline directory navigation UI for creating new sessions, plus window pickers and session pickers (with resume support). history.py implements paginated message history with byte-range-aware navigation. cleanup.py handles topic state cleanup on close. Follow the data flow: status_polling detects changes → interactive_ui renders prompts → message_queue enqueues updates → message_sender delivers to Telegram.

tour.title: Infrastructure & Cross-Cutting Concerns
tour.description: The project infrastructure ties everything together. pyproject.toml declares dependencies (python-telegram-bot, libtmux, aiofiles, ruff, pyright). The .env.example shows required configuration (TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, ALLOWED_USERS, OPENAI_API_KEY). hook.py implements the SessionStart hook that writes session_map.json — this is the critical contract between Claude Code and CCBot that enables session tracking. utils.py provides shared utilities (atomic_write_json, ccbot_dir). transcribe.py handles voice message transcription via OpenAI API. The cross-cutting patterns to note: MarkdownV2-only with fallback, FIFO message queues with merging, hook-based session tracking, and 1-topic=1-window routing without backward-compat for non-topic mode.
